title: Reina Valera New Testament of the Bible 1909
summaries: "Reina Valera New Testament of the Bible 1909" by Reina and Valera is a biblical translation revised in 1909. Born from Casiodoro de Reina's 1569 translation and Cipriano de Valera's 1602 revision, this Spanish Bible became the primary Protestant scripture for over four centuries. Translated directly from ancient Hebrew and Greek texts rather than Latin, it shaped spiritual life across Spanish-speaking Protestant communities. The 1909 edition represents one of several revisions that adapted this foundational work for modern readers while preserving its linguistic heritage. (This is an automatically generated summary.)
